chunk_summary: Guidelines for obesity comorbidity screening and identifying secondary obesity red flags, emphasizing age-specific evaluations, risk factors, and practical screening protocols tailored to India.
learning_objectives: Understand age-specific guidelines for obesity comorbidity screening in children.
Identify key risk factors and red flags for secondary obesity requiring referral.
Learn practical, cost-effective screening protocols for Indian clinical settings.
Incorporate psychosocial and developmental assessments into obesity management.
key_facts: The IAP guideline recommends comorbidity screening for children with obesity aged ≥10 years and for ages 2–10 years if risk factors (e.g., family history of diabetes, hypertension) or waist circumference >70th percentile are present.
AAP guidelines suggest fasting lipid panels for children ≥10 years with obesity or overweight, and discretionary lipid evaluation for ages 2–9 years with obesity.
Core screening includes BP measurement, fasting lipid profile, fasting glucose and/or HbA1c, and ALT for NAFLD/MASLD.
Red flags for secondary obesity include poor height velocity, obesity onset before age 5 with hyperphagia, developmental delay, or dysmorphism.
Psychosocial factors like bullying or depression and menstrual history in adolescents are critical for adherence to treatment.
reference_quote: The IAP obesity guideline recommends comorbidity evaluation for all children with obesity aged 10 years and above, and for children aged 2–10 years with obesity when risk factors are present or waist circumference is above the 70th percentile.
